messages.content:
I'm going through some things with my feelings and myself. I barely sleep and I do nothing but think about how I'm worthless and how I shouldn't be here.
   I've never tried or contemplated suicide. I've always wanted to fix my issues, but I never get around to it.
   How can I change my feeling of being worthless to everyone?

Hello, and thank you for your question and seeking advice on this. Feelings of worthlessness is unfortunately common. In fact, most people, if not all, have felt this to some degree at some point in their life. You are not alone. Changing our feelings is like changing our thoughts - it's hard to do. Our minds are so amazing that the minute you change your thought another one can be right there to take it's place. Without your permission, another thought can just pop in there. The new thought may feel worse than the last one! My guess is that you have tried several things to improve this on your own even before reaching out on here. People often try thinking positive thoughts, debating with their thoughts, or simply telling themselves that they need to "snap out of it" - which is also a thought that carries some self-criticism. Some people try a different approach, and there are counselors out there that can help you with this. The idea is that instead of trying to change the thoughts, you change how you respond to them. You learn skills that allow you to manage difficult thoughts and feelings differently so they don't have the same impact on you that they do right now. For some people, they actually DO begin to experience less hurtful thoughts once they learn how to manage the ones they have differently. Acceptance and Commitment Therapy may be a good choice for you. There is information online and even self-help books that you can use to teach you the skills that I mentioned. Because they are skills, they require practice, but many people have found great relief and an enriched life by learning them. As for suicidal thoughts, I am very glad to read that this has not happened to you. Still, you should watch out for this because it can be a sign of a worsening depression. If you begin to think about this, it is important to reach out to a support system right away. The National Suicide Prevention Lifeline is 1-800-273-8255. The text line is #741741. I hope some other colleagues will provide you more suggestions. Be well...Robin Landwehr, DBH, LPCC

evidence: Changing our feelings is like changing our thoughts - it's hard to do.
Acceptance and Commitment Therapy may be a good choice for you.
There is information online and even self-help books that you can use.
interactionPattern.rationale: AI provides structured guidance, recommendations (ACT), and counsel throughout. Human seeks advice on a personal problem; AI responds with prescriptive recommendations and coping strategies.

evidence: AI frames the problem, explains mechanisms, and prescribes approaches.
AI introduces ACT as a specific recommendation.
AI provides crisis resources and directs future action.
powerDynamics.rationale: AI controls the conversational direction, introduces frameworks, and structures the response. Human initiated but AI dominates through authoritative guidance and topic framing.
powerDynamics.alternative: balanced (if viewing human's initial vulnerability as equal conversational weight)

evidence: Feelings of worthlessness is unfortunately common. You are not alone.
I am very glad to read that this has not happened to you.
I hope some other colleagues will provide you more suggestions. Be well.
emotionalTone.rationale: AI validates feelings, normalizes experience, expresses concern, and closes with warmth. Tone balances professional expertise with compassionate acknowledgment of human struggle.

evidence: You are not alone.
Many people have found great relief and an enriched life by learning them.
Thank you for your question and seeking advice on this.
engagementStyle.rationale: AI validates human's experience, affirms their effort to seek help, and builds on their stated desire to fix issues. Does not challenge or question; instead supports and extends.
engagementStyle.alternative: directive-iterative (if emphasizing the prescriptive structure of recommendations)

evidence: You learn skills that allow you to manage difficult thoughts and feelings differently.
Because they are skills, they require practice.
There is information online and even self-help books that you can use to teach you the skills.
knowledgeExchange.rationale: AI teaches coping mechanisms and therapeutic approaches (ACT, thought management) rather than just providing facts. Focus is on learnable techniques and methods.
knowledgeExchange.alternative: meaning-making (given existential nature of worthlessness exploration)

evidence: I'm going through some things with my feelings and myself.
I barely sleep and I do nothing but think about how I'm worthless.
How can I change my feeling of being worthless to everyone?
conversationPurpose.rationale: Human is processing difficult emotions and seeking to work through feelings of worthlessness. While problem-solving is present, the primary purpose is emotional exploration and validation of internal struggle.
conversationPurpose.alternative: problem-solving (if emphasizing the concrete recommendations for change)

evidence: AI explains cognitive mechanisms: 'the minute you change your thought another one can be right there.'
AI introduces specific therapeutic framework (ACT) with explanation of how it works.
AI addresses both immediate concerns and warning signs of escalation.
topicDepth.rationale: Despite single exchange, AI provides nuanced exploration of thought patterns, therapeutic mechanisms, and differentiated approaches. Moves beyond surface reassurance to substantive explanation.

evidence: Human message: ~80 words
AI message: ~450 words (approximately 5.6x longer)
turnTaking.rationale: AI response is substantially longer and more detailed than human's initial message. Single exchange makes this clear.

evidence.quote: I'm going through some things with my feelings and myself. I barely sleep and I do nothing but think about how I'm worthless.
How can I change my feeling of being worthless to everyone?
I've always wanted to fix my issues, but I never get around to it.

humanRole.rationale: Human primarily expresses internal emotional struggle and personal narrative (social-expressor dominant). Secondary role as information-seeker asking for specific guidance. Minimal relational-peer quality given vulnerability context.
humanRole.alternative: provider (if viewing human as seeking to provide information about their condition to AI)

evidence.quote: Acceptance and Commitment Therapy may be a good choice for you. The National Suicide Prevention Lifeline is 1-800-273-8255.
You learn skills that allow you to manage difficult thoughts and feelings differently. Because they are skills, they require practice.
There is information online and even self-help books that you can use to teach you the skills.
You are not alone. I am very glad to read that this has not happened to you.

aiRole.rationale: AI primarily asserts expertise through framework introduction and resource provision (expert-system). Secondary learning-facilitator role in explaining skill-based approaches. Advisor role in prescribing ACT and self-help. Minimal social-facilitator warmth given clinical tone.
aiRole.alternative: advisor-dominant (if emphasizing prescriptive recommendations over explanatory scaffolding)
